title: Isolation of Circular Single-Stranded DNA from Total DNA in Grapevine Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ssmb","text":"This protocol details a method for the purification and enrichment of circular single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) viruses from total DNA extractions. Through a series of enzymatic reactions, the protocol is optimized to selectively enrich the native form of circular ssDNA viruses, preserving chemical modifications such as methylation on their genomes. The expected results include highly purified viral DNA, enabling full-length sequencing of the circular ssDNA virus and the detection of methylation patterns. This makes the protocol ideal for studying both the genome structure and epigenetic modifications of circular ssDNA viruses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gcuo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"du6pn","text":"Prepare all buffers and enzyme mixes fresh before use to ensure optimal enzyme activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"al8tc","text":"Use sterile, DNase-free water and tubes to avoid contamination of the DNA sample.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgmri","text":"Ensure accurate pipetting of enzymes and reaction components, as enzyme activity depends on precise reaction conditions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsif3","text":"Incubation times and temperatures must be strictly followed for efficient digestion and processing of the DNA.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vsdm","text":"Bead washing and elution steps should be performed gently to prevent DNA loss or damage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dlhas","text":"Confirm the total DNA concentration is sufficient for the reaction (we used ~22 µg for initial digestion).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"336tp","text":"Prepare the following buffers and enzyme mixes:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nt45","text":"rCutSmart‱ Buffer for restriction enzyme digestion.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":9,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6vndk","text":"E. coli DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer for polymerase activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"69pqk","text":"Preheat the incubators or water baths to 37°C for restriction digestion and 25°C for exonuclease reactions.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":41,"length":4},{"style":"bold","offset":76,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1avqr","text":"Have bead washing and elution materials ready, ensuring the beads are appropriately equilibrated for efficient DNA recovery.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"793CA651C7BD11EFAADC0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"em0a1","text":"Always wear gloves and use a fume hood when handling enzymes, DTT, and other reagents to avoid contamination and exposure.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ohl2","text":"Restriction enzymes and exonucleases are temperature-sensitive; ensure proper storage and handling to maintain activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58kll","text":"Dispose of all biohazardous waste, such as used tips and tubes, according to your institution's biosafety protocols.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"456h2","text":"Bead washing steps must be performed carefully to prevent DNA loss; do not overdry or overwash the beads.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50oke","text":"Validate digestion efficiency (e.g., Qubit) before proceeding to ensure the protocol's success.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the Reaction Mixture: Combine the following in a microcentrifuge tube: %s %s

This step removes all linear DNA fragments (both double-stranded and single-stranded) that were generated during the restriction digestion step. The remaining DNA will predominantly consist of circular single-stranded DNA (ssDNA). %s %s Explanation: This step leaves only circular DNA intact, ensuring that no linear DNA contaminants interfere with downstream applications. Circular single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) remains protected from exonuclease activity due to its structure.

Do Bead washing to remove any residual contaminants and purifies the remaining DNA. In all bead washing steps performed in this protocol, 1.5X ratio of beads relative to the volume of the DNA solution was used to ensure efficient purification and recovery of DNA. %s %s

This step synthesizes the complementary strand for circular single-stranded DNA (ssDNA), effectively creating double-stranded DNA (dsDNA). %s %s Set Up Reaction: Combine the above components in a clean microcentrifuge tube. Ensure the DNA, buffer, and enzymes are mixed gently to avoid damage to the DNA. Incubate the reaction at 16°C for 2.5 hour. Point : During this incubation, Klenow DNA Polymerase I synthesizes the complementary strand of the circular ssDNA. E. coli DNA Ligase seals the nicks to form complete double-stranded DNA. Bead Washing and Elution: Use magnetic beads to purify the synthesized dsDNA. Elute in an appropriate volume of DNase-free water (typically 50 µL).

This step was performed to produce linear double-stranded DNA from the GRBV genome. One strand of the resulting DNA represents the native strand of the circular ssDNA, while the other strand is the complementary sequence synthesized during the Klenow reaction. The digestion by KpnI ensures the DNA is linearized, which is necessary because the Nanopore library preparation kits require linear double-stranded DNA as input material for sequencing. %s %s Combine the components in a clean microcentrifuge tube. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hour to allow digestion by KpnI. Final Bead Washing (Critical Step): Use magnetic beads to wash the reaction mixture thoroughly, ensuring all residual enzymes and buffer components are removed. Elute the purified linear DNA for further analysis. Point: We used KpnI because it is the one of restriction enzymes that cuts the GRBV genome at a single site, enabling precise linearization and having the full length of GRBV. Users of this protocol should select a restriction enzyme that cuts their viral genome at one specific site to achieve accurate linearization for downstream library preparation and sequencing. Measure DNA Concentration Use Qubit to measure DNA concentration; in our case yield was ~4 µg.
